hypothesis: Generated-schema normalization before G11, combined with optional-null semantics from required metadata, will retain legal training pairs while rejecting true value-role violations; semantic quality must then be measured independently from syntax.

correction.false_positive_cause: The resolved AST uses null as the omission sentinel for optional positional properties before later required properties.

invalidated_preflight.reason: Schema normalization was not yet applied before G11; the untracked snapshot was deleted before training.

implementation.judge: Optional null is accepted only when the property is absent from generated required metadata; parser semantic errors, enums, refs, unions, arrays, objects, and scalar types remain checked.
implementation.normalization: Every train-data candidate passes through canonical schema normalization before verification. Enum defaults and array container shapes come from library_schema.
implementation.producer: The language-contract builder recursively selects a generated anyOf branch, so FormControl.input now receives an Input binder instead of a placeholder string.
implementation.canonical_seeds: Form and gallery prompts now match their generated component structures; train and test seed golds pass the independent judge before future builds.

conclusion: Corrected admission restores 33 valid records and keeps deterministic syntax at 1.0, but E220 exactly matches E216's semantic metrics: component recall 0.25 and meaningful parse 0.0. Data admission is now sounder; it does not solve semantic coverage at this budget. No checkpoint is promotable.
next_hypothesis: Measure prompt-requested generated-schema role coverage across admitted records and derive schema-valid positives for underrepresented requested component-role sets; retain a frozen nearby counterexample set for G11 without using invalid outputs as training targets.
